017package org.apache.juneau.marshall.csv;
018
019import java.util.*;
020
021import org.apache.juneau.marshall.parser.*;
022
023/**
024 * Parses CSV cell inline notation: {@code {key:val;key2:val2}} and {@code [val1;val2;val3]}.
025 *
026 * <p>
027 * Grammar (CSV-specific, semicolon-separated):
028 * <ul>
029 *   <li>Object: {@code \{ (key ':' value (';' key ':' value)*)? \}}
030 *   <li>Array: {@code \[ (value (';' value)*)? \]}
031 *   <li>Values: object | array | quoted string | number | identifier | null
032 * </ul>
033 *
034 * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
035 *    <li class='note'>Reserved characters: {@code ; : \{ \} [ ] " \}
036 * </ul>
037 */
038public final class CsvCellParser {
039
040   private final String input;
041   private final String nullMarker;
042   private int pos;
043   private int length;
044
045   private CsvCellParser(String input, String nullMarker) {
046      this.input = input != null ? input.trim() : "";
047      this.nullMarker = nullMarker != null ? nullMarker : "null";
048      this.length = this.input.length();
049   }
050
051   /**
052    * Parses a cell value string into a Map, List, or scalar.
053    *
054    * @param cell The cell string (e.g. {@code {a:1;b:2}} or {@code [1;2;3]}).
055    * @param nullMarker The string that denotes null (e.g. {@code <NULL>} or {@code null}).
056    * @return Parsed object: Map, List, String, Number, Boolean, or null.
057    * @throws ParseException If the input is invalid.
058    */
059   public static Object parse(String cell, String nullMarker) throws ParseException {
060      if (cell == null || cell.isEmpty())
061         return null;
062      var p = new CsvCellParser(cell, nullMarker);
063      try {
064         return p.parseValue();
065      } catch (Exception e) {
066         throw new ParseException(e, "Invalid CSV cell notation at position {0}: ''{1}''", p.pos, cell);
067      }
068   }
069
070   private void skipWhitespace() {
071      while (pos < length && Character.isWhitespace(input.charAt(pos)))
072         pos++;
073   }
074
075   private boolean hasMore() {
076      skipWhitespace();
077      return pos < length;
078   }
079
080   private char peek() {
081      return pos < length ? input.charAt(pos) : '\0';
082   }
083
084   private char consume() {
085      return pos < length ? input.charAt(pos++) : '\0';
086   }
087
088   private Object parseValue() throws ParseException {
089      skipWhitespace();
090      if (pos >= length)
091         return null;
092      var c = peek();
093      if (c == '{')
094         return parseObject();
095      if (c == '[')
096         return parseArray();
097      if (c == '"')
098         return parseQuotedString();
099      return parseSimpleValue();
100   }
101
102   private Map<String, Object> parseObject() throws ParseException {
103      if (consume() != '{')
104         throw new ParseException("Expected opening brace");
105      var m = new LinkedHashMap<String, Object>();
106      skipWhitespace();
107      if (peek() == '}') {
108         consume();
109         return m;
110      }
111      while (hasMore()) {
112         var key = parseKey();
113         skipWhitespace();
114         if (consume() != ':')
115            throw new ParseException("Expected ':' after key at position " + pos);
116         var value = parseValue();
117         m.put(key, value);
118         skipWhitespace();
119         var c = peek();
120         if (c == '}') {
121            consume();
122            break;
123         }
124         if (c != ';')
125            throw new ParseException("Expected ';' or '}' at position " + pos);
126         consume(); // skip ';'
127      }
128      return m;
129   }
130
131   private String parseKey() throws ParseException {
132      skipWhitespace();
133      if (pos >= length)
134         throw new ParseException("Unexpected end of input in key");
135      if (peek() == '"')
136         return parseQuotedString();
137      return parseIdentifier();
138   }
139
140   private List<Object> parseArray() throws ParseException {
141      if (consume() != '[')
142         throw new ParseException("Expected opening bracket");
143      var list = new ArrayList<>();
144      skipWhitespace();
145      if (peek() == ']') {
146         consume();
147         return list;
148      }
149      while (hasMore()) {
150         list.add(parseValue());
151         skipWhitespace();
152         var c = peek();
153         if (c == ']') {
154            consume();
155            break;
156         }
157         if (c != ';')
158            throw new ParseException("Expected ';' or ']' at position " + pos);
159         consume(); // skip ';'
160      }
161      return list;
162   }
163
164   private String parseQuotedString() throws ParseException {
165      if (consume() != '"')
166         throw new ParseException("Expected quote");
167      var sb = new StringBuilder();
168      while (pos < length) {
169         var c = consume();
170         if (c == '"')
171            return sb.toString();
172         if (c == '\\' && pos < length)
173            sb.append(consume()); // escaped char
174         else
175            sb.append(c);
176      }
177      throw new ParseException("Unterminated quoted string");
178   }
179
180   private String parseIdentifier() {
181      var start = pos;
182      while (pos < length) {
183         var c = input.charAt(pos);
184         if (c == ';' || c == ':' || c == '{' || c == '}' || c == '[' || c == ']' || c == '"' || Character.isWhitespace(c))
185            break;
186         pos++;
187      }
188      return input.substring(start, pos);
189   }
190
191   private Object parseSimpleValue() throws ParseException {
192      skipWhitespace();
193      if (pos >= length || peek() == ';' || peek() == '}' || peek() == ']')
194         return "";
195      var id = parseIdentifier();
196      if (id.isEmpty())
197         throw new ParseException("Expected value at position " + pos);
198      if (id.equalsIgnoreCase(nullMarker))
199         return null;
200      if (id.equals("true"))
201         return Boolean.TRUE;
202      if (id.equals("false"))
203         return Boolean.FALSE;
204      // Try number
205      try {
206         if (id.contains(".")) {
207            var d = Double.parseDouble(id);
208            if (d == (long) d)
209               return Long.valueOf((long) d);
210            return Double.valueOf(d);
211         }
212         return Long.valueOf(id);
213      } catch (@SuppressWarnings("unused") NumberFormatException e) {
214         // Not a number, treat as string
215         return id;
216      }
217   }
218}
